title: Complete List of S&P 500 Dividend Aristocrats
description: Discover the definitive 2026 S&P 500 Dividend Aristocrats list. See which top companies have consistently raised their dividends for 25+ years and how their yields compare.

mainEntity.name: What is the complete list of S&P 500 dividend aristocrats as of march 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: The complete list includes 69 companies from the S&P 500 that have increased their base dividend every year for at least 25 consecutive years, including stalwarts like Johnson & Johnson, Coca-Cola, and Procter & Gamble.

mainEntity.name: What is the average dividend yield of S&P 500 dividend aristocrats in 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: As of early 2026, the average dividend yield among the Dividend Aristocrats is approximately 2.55%, compared to the S&P 500 average yield of 1.06%.

mainEntity.name: How many companies are on the dividend aristocrats list in 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: There are exactly 69 companies on the Dividend Aristocrats list for 2026.

mainEntity.name: What are the requirements to be a Dividend Aristocrat?

acceptedAnswer.text: To be classified as a Dividend Aristocrat, a company must be a member of the S&P 500 index, have increased its base dividend payout every year for at least 25 consecutive years, and meet certain market capitalization and liquidity requirements.

mainEntity.name: Which sectors have the most Dividend Aristocrats?

acceptedAnswer.text: Industrials, Consumer Staples (Defensive), and Materials traditionally contain the most Dividend Aristocrats due to their stable, cash-generating business models across economic cycles.
